title: Listening - Part 4: Career Changes into Teaching
description: You are going to read a magazine article about an intellectual process known as critical thinking. For questions 31 – 36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text. Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
topic: five individuals discussing why they left successful careers to become teachers

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I’d been a corporate lawyer for fifteen years, but [21]I reached a point where the astronomical salary just didn't compensate for the lack of social purpose in my work[/21]. I wanted to contribute to society. [26]The most rewarding aspect has been the unexpected boost in my own creativity[/26]. Designing lesson plans that engage thirty teenagers requires a level of imagination I never used in law. It’s revitalized my brain. || Speaker 2: I was a software engineer, but [22]I decided to teach because I wanted to encourage more girls to enter the tech industry and bridge the gender gap[/22]. I wanted to be a role model. However, [27]the biggest challenge was the sheer physical exhaustion of being on my feet all day[/27]. In my old job, I sat at a desk; now, I’m constantly moving. It took months for my body to adjust to the pace. || Speaker 3: I’d spent my career in marketing, but [23]I moved into teaching after a personal health scare made me re-evaluate my work-life balance and stress levels[/23]. I needed a change. [28]What I didn't expect was the profound sense of belonging I’ve found in the staff room[/28]. My old office was hyper-competitive, but here, everyone supports each other. It’s a true community. || Speaker 4: I’m a former research scientist, and [24]I became a teacher primarily because I was tired of the isolation of the lab and missed human interaction[/24]. I wanted to share my passion for science. [29]The most difficult hurdle has been the amount of administrative paperwork involved[/29]. I thought I’d be spending all my time teaching, but the reports and assessments are endless. It’s a struggle to keep up. || Speaker 5: I’d been a journalist, but [25]I chose teaching because I wanted a career with more stability and a predictable routine for my family[/25]. I was tired of traveling. [30]The most significant outcome has been the massive improvement in my public speaking and presentation skills[/30]. Even after years in news, I’ve learned so much about how to communicate complex ideas clearly and persuasively to a skeptical audience. It’s been a great growth experience.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21–25, choose from the list (A–H) what primarily motivated each speaker to change careers.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 wanted more social purpose in her work than her law career provided.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 wanted to act as a role model and encourage girls in tech.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 changed careers after a health scare made her re-evaluate her life.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 missed human interaction after years in an isolated lab.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 wanted more stability and a predictable routine for his family.

options.A: to fulfill a long-held childhood ambition
options.B: to escape a solitary working environment
options.D: to pursue a higher academic degree
options.E: to find more meaning in their professional life
options.F: to respond to a change in their health
options.H: to achieve a better domestic routine

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26–30, choose from the list (A–H) what each speaker mentions about their new life as a teacher.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 found that designing lessons gave her an unexpected creative boost.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 mentions the physical exhaustion of being on her feet all day.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 found a supportive community and sense of belonging among staff.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 found the administrative paperwork and reports a difficult hurdle.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 notes an improvement in his ability to communicate complex ideas clearly.

options.A: a sense of regret about the salary drop
options.C: the discovery of a more imaginative side
options.D: a noticeable development in their communication
options.E: the pleasure of seeing student progress
options.F: the physical strain of the new role
options.G: frustration with the level of bureaucracy
options.H: the value of a collaborative atmosphere
